title: Malaxis lombasangensis
taxonomy: Malaxis lombasangensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a member of the subtribe Malaxidinae, which includes several species of terrestrial orchids. The genus Malaxis is a group of small to medium-sized orchids, often found in woodland environments. Malaxis lombasangensis is a relatively rare species, endemic to the Philippines. Further classification includes the order Asparagales, which is a large group of flowering plants that includes orchids and several other families.

intro: Malaxis lombasangensis, also known as the Lombasang orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae), characterized by its delicate white flowers and unique growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis lombasangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Malaxis lombasangensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
